title: Empirical validation spike for Tactus-Lua MCP
description:
# Empirical validation spike for Tactus-Lua MCP

## Purpose

GATING. Validate that frontier LLMs can write decent Plexus-flavored Tactus Lua against a small boot prompt + on-demand docs, end-to-end through the actual Tactus runtime. Determines whether to commit to the rest of the initiative.

## Why this is gating

The entire initiative bets on the hypothesis that current frontier models can write correct Plexus-flavored Tactus Lua from a small boot prompt. If that hypothesis is wrong, the architecture is wrong, and we need to know in a week not a quarter. Three months of work spent before validating this would be a major project failure.

## Method

- Build a stub `PlexusModule` (read-only operations only — `scorecards.list`, `score.info`, `score.predict`, `feedback.find`, `evaluation.info`, `docs.get`, `docs.list`, `budget.remaining`).
- Stub a long-running operation (`evaluation.run`) with simulated streaming progress and a synthetic budget meter, to validate the dispatch-side ergonomics without building real infrastructure.
- Initial boot prompt: ≤2k tokens.
- Test 10–15 representative real-world Plexus tasks against multiple frontier models.
- Models: claude-4.6-sonnet, claude-4.7-opus, gpt-5.3-codex, gemini-3.1-pro.
- Capture full transcripts: prompts, generated Lua, errors, retries, final results.
- Measure: first-try success rate, iterations to working code, total token usage including tool definitions, time-to-correct-result, latency, cost, debugability.

## Pass / fail criterion

≥85% first-try success rate across the representative task set, on at least 2 of the 4 models tested.

## Failure analysis

If we fail the gate:
- Determine whether failure is due to API design (fixable), boot prompt content (fixable), Lua language choice (architectural), or fundamental "models can't do code mode for this domain" (project-killer).
- Propose specific design changes and re-spike, OR escalate to "this architectural direction is wrong" report.

## Deliverable

A go/no-go report with quantitative results per model per task, qualitative analysis of failure patterns, and a recommendation. Shared with stakeholders before any other epic begins implementation work.

## Status

Stories and tasks fleshed out below — this epic is the immediate next work.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
# Spike readiness update

Current branch: `feature/runtime-mcp-validation-tasks`. No commits made.

## Complete and verified in working tree

- `plx-7041b9` curated 15 representative spike task YAML files plus coverage README under `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/tasks/`.
- `plx-f44454` added fixture-backed `PlexusModule` stub and deterministic fixture data.
- `plx-a35142` added simulated long-running `plexus.evaluation.run` with streaming events, async handles, cancellation, and budget exhaustion behavior.
- `plx-8c6855` added compact `boot_prompt.md`; measured earlier at 772 `cl100k_base` tokens, below the 2k spike limit.
- `plx-952377` added harness with Lupa execution shim, provider adapter entry points, readiness checks, result writing, and deterministic `stub-oracle` validation.

## Current blocker

- `plx-724038` remains blocked on provider credentials/configuration. Provider packages are present, but credentials are absent:
  - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=false`
  - `OPENAI_API_KEY=false`
  - LiteLLM-supported env vars absent: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `VERTEXAI_PROJECT`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`
- No real model calls have been run.

## Verification run from parent agent

```bash
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --validate-tasks
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --check-providers
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-parent-verify
```

Results:
- Task validation: 15 tasks valid.
- Provider readiness check: packages present, credentials missing, ready=false for anthropic/openai/litellm.
- Stub-oracle smoke: passed 15/15.

## Status decision

I am leaving completed implementation tasks in their current `in_progress` state rather than closing them because the deliverables are verified in the working tree but have not been reviewed/landed/committed. `plx-724038` must remain blocked until credentials are configured and at least a one-task real-provider readiness run succeeds.

## Next unblocked step

There is no final go/no-go report to produce yet. `plx-8e009e` should remain pending until real-provider results from `plx-724038` exist. The next actionable step is to configure provider credentials, then run one low-cost readiness command before any full matrix:

```bash
python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py \
  --task predict_single_item \
  --model litellm:<configured-low-cost-model> \
  --run-id provider-readiness-001
```
